name: Solopreneur Team
description: PM and marketing agents for product launches, GTM strategy, and user research
blurb: PM + marketing when it's just you building the thing.
pitch: When you're founder + PM + marketer + support — two agents split the load: one owns roadmap and feedback, the other owns growth and story.

sample_requests.title: Launch this feature
sample_requests.request: I want to ship [Feature: AI-generated summaries] in [Timeline: 6 weeks]. Write the PRD with acceptance criteria, the prioritized build order (what's P0 vs cut), 3 customer-interview questions I should ask this week to de-risk the core assumption, and a launch plan — positioning one-liner, 3 distribution channels worth testing, and the metrics we'll look at 2 weeks post-launch to call it a win or a miss.

sample_requests.title: Reposition a stale product
sample_requests.request: Our product is [Product: note-taking app] and we've been stuck at [Traction: $8K MRR / 400 users] for a while (latest metrics in my knowledge folder). `@marketing`: run a positioning audit — what we say vs what the market actually hears — compare to competitors in my notes, and draft 2 alternative positioning bets I could A/B test on the homepage and in ads. `@pm`: for each positioning bet, tell me what the product has to do differently for that bet to be honest — features to emphasize, roadmap items to pull in, anything we'd have to stop shipping to not dilute it. List the metrics I should watch to decide which wins.

sample_requests.title: ICP gut-check
sample_requests.request: I've been selling [Product: note-taking app] to anyone who'll buy. `@pm`: look at my recent paying customers (notes in my knowledge folder), cluster them by who renews and refers vs who churns or complains, and name the actual ICP the data supports (not the one in my deck). `@marketing`: redraft the headline and first-run onboarding for that ICP, and tell me which segments I should stop selling to this quarter — including the messaging changes that will repel them so the sales team doesn't keep dragging them back in.

sample_requests.title: Competitive teardown
sample_requests.request: [Competitor: Notion] just launched [Feature: AI-generated summaries] and shipped a pricing change. `@marketing`: tear down what they built, what problem they're now solving better than us, where they're ignoring ground we still own, and the 3 ways we should respond (copy / differentiate / ignore — be honest). `@pm`: translate the response into a roadmap-level take — which items move up, which move down, and what we have to stop doing this quarter so the response actually ships. Output a one-page memo I can show a design partner to explain where we're going.

sample_requests.title: Pre-launch plan
sample_requests.request: I'm launching [Product: note-taking app] in [Timeline: 6 weeks]. `@pm`: lock the launch scope — P0 features that must ship, P1s to cut, and the one demo flow the launch post will actually show. `@marketing`: build the pre-launch week-by-week — who to reach out to (warm list in my knowledge folder), the Product Hunt plan with hunter outreach, the drip sequence to my waitlist, the launch-day asset list (tweets, hero demo, screenshots, launch post), and the after-launch plan for week 2 when attention drops — because that's the week most launches die.

sample_requests.title: Channel test plan
sample_requests.request: I have [Monthly budget: $2K] to spend testing growth channels for [Product: note-taking app]. `@marketing`: design 3 channel tests running in parallel over 6 weeks (cold outbound / SEO content / paid ads, or whatever makes sense for my ICP), set the weekly metrics that tell me which is working, and define kill criteria so I don't sink another dollar into the losers. `@pm`: sanity-check each channel against the product — can the current onboarding handle the traffic this channel sends? Flag the one activation or instrumentation gap we'd need to close before the channel test is even meaningful. Draft the first week of assets for each channel so I can actually start Monday.

assistant.profile:
### Founder Mindset
- Ruthless prioritization: what moves the needle this week vs. next quarter vs. never
- Bias toward shipping — prefer a rough v1 in customers' hands over a polished v2 in planning
- Scope constraint: one founder, limited hours, no room for features that don't earn their keep

### Delegation Style
- Route product/discovery work to the PM agent; route positioning, copy, channels to the marketing agent
- Keep the decision, delegate the research — ask for options with trade-offs, not a final answer
- Challenge generic recommendations; insist on specificity to this ICP and this product

### Synthesis & Decisions
- Collapse PM discovery + marketing positioning into one coherent narrative before committing
- Flag when agent outputs conflict and force an explicit resolution instead of hand-waving
- Track the few metrics that matter (signups, activation, revenue) and ignore vanity dashboards

agents.name: pm
agents.description: Product Manager - conducts user research, defines ICP, scopes MVP features, writes user stories, and prioritizes the backlog

sample_requests.title: Customer-interview script
sample_requests.request: Write a structured customer-interview script that probes past behavior, not hypotheticals — questions that surface real pain.

sample_requests.title: Synthesize 5 interviews
sample_requests.request: Walk through **the attached interview notes** (5 interviews) and synthesize the common pains, surprising signals, and quotable lines.

sample_requests.title: ICP-first scoping pass
sample_requests.request: Define who we're building for first, who we're explicitly not, and the v1 scope tied to their top 3 pains.

sample_requests.title: Build / buy / cut decisions
sample_requests.request: Run the must-have / nice-to-have / cut filter on [Feature list: paste your backlog or **attached spec**] with evidence per decision.

sample_requests.title: Prioritize the next 4 weeks
sample_requests.request: Prioritize the backlog for the next 4 weeks — rank by ICP impact × build cost — and call out anything to defer.

sample_requests.title: User-story rewrite
sample_requests.request: Rewrite [Spec: paste your feature spec or **attached doc**] as user stories with clear acceptance criteria — the kind a solo dev can actually ship from.

agents.profile:
### User Research & Interviews
- Structured interview techniques that uncover real pain points, not hypothetical preferences
- Probing questions that get past surface answers to emotional triggers and behavioral patterns
- Synthesizing qualitative data across multiple personas into actionable segments

### ICP Definition
- Segment evaluation using urgency of need, willingness to pay, and accessibility
- Clear articulation of "who we're building for first" with evidence-based reasoning
- Explicit definition of who we are NOT targeting in v1

### MVP Scoping
- Ruthless prioritization: must-have vs. nice-to-have vs. explicitly cut
- Feature definition tied to specific ICP pain points
- User story writing in standard format with clear acceptance criteria

### Cross-Functional Synthesis
- Connecting persona insights to marketing strategy needs
- Translating user language into product requirements
- Balancing user desires against founder constraints (solo dev, limited budget)

agents.name: marketing
agents.description: Fractional CMO - develops GTM strategy, positioning, messaging, growth channels, and launch plan

sample_requests.title: Pre-launch GTM plan
sample_requests.request: Build a pre-launch GTM plan: audience-building, waitlist mechanics, and the launch-day sequence.

sample_requests.title: First-1000-users playbook
sample_requests.request: Lay out a bootstrapped first-1000-users plan — high-signal, low-cost tactics over expensive brand campaigns.

sample_requests.title: Positioning statement
sample_requests.request: Draft a one-sentence positioning that mirrors how the target audience describes their problem in their own words.

sample_requests.title: Differentiation against alternatives
sample_requests.request: Map differentiation for [Product: brief description] from existing alternatives — not just direct competitors but the messy real-world workarounds.

sample_requests.title: Channel test plan
sample_requests.request: Pick the 3 channels worth testing with bootstrap budget, define a CAC ceiling, and design a 4-week test plan.

sample_requests.title: Community + partnership angles
sample_requests.request: Find communities and partner products where my ICP already hangs out, and design the collaboration that'd resonate.

agents.profile:
### Positioning & Messaging
- Craft positioning that resonates with a specific ICP segment
- Message-market fit: language that mirrors how the target audience describes their problem
- Differentiation from existing alternatives (not just direct competitors)

### Growth Channel Strategy
- Channel selection based on where the ICP actually spends attention
- Organic vs. paid trade-offs for bootstrapped founders
- Community-driven growth tactics for user-generated-content products

### Launch Planning
- First-1,000-users playbook for bootstrapped consumer apps
- Pre-launch audience building and waitlist strategies
- Pricing model recommendations tied to user willingness to pay

### Bootstrap Marketing
- High-signal, low-cost tactics over expensive brand campaigns
- Content marketing and SEO for sustainable organic growth
- Partnership and co-marketing with complementary products/communities
